Fontbrunea lucens, also known as the "bushy helichrysum," is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Fontbrunea lucens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Fontbrunea lucens include essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and respiratory issues.
